Entity_Description: Port Coquitlam is a municipality of 61,498 people (2021 Census) in Metro Vancouver, British Columbia, about 25 km east of Vancouver in the Tri-Cities. Incorporated in 1913 as a Canadian Pacific Railway yard town, it sits where the Coquitlam River meets the Fraser, with the Pitt River on its eastern edge. Known as "PoCo" and as the hometown of Terry Fox, it is ringed by the roughly 25-kilometre Traboulay PoCo Trail and blends an older Shaughnessy Street downtown with neighbourhoods like Mary Hill, Citadel Heights and Riverwood.

Short_Description: Port Coquitlam grew up around the Canadian Pacific Railway yards, and more than a century after its 1913 incorporation, PoCo still works like a rail town: practical, tight-knit, and proud, the hometown of Terry Fox. It is also a city defined by three rivers. The Coquitlam River splits it down the middle, the Fraser runs along its south edge and the Pitt frames the east, which means everything from hillside homes in Citadel Heights to older bungalows near downtown Shaughnessy Street deals with serious winter water. That is exactly the environment {COMPANY_NAME} built its {SERVICE} around. As a {BUSINESS_TYPE} working across all 61,498 residents' worth of PoCo neighbourhoods, we match the service to the street, not the other way around. Get your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} today. Call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Port Coquitlam has never pretended to be anything other than a working city. It was incorporated in 1913 as a Canadian Pacific Railway yard town, and the rail lands along the Fraser River still shape how the city moves and where its industry sits. That heritage matters for {SERVICE}, because PoCo's building stock tells the whole story of its growth: modest older homes in Glenwood and Birchland Manor near the original townsite, postwar family houses climbing Mary Hill and Oxford Heights, newer hillside subdivisions in Citadel Heights, and the master-planned Riverwood and Fremont area on the east side, where townhouse and strata living is now a defining feature, just as it is across Metro Vancouver.

Geography is the other half of the PoCo story. This is a city framed by three rivers. The Coquitlam River cuts north to south through the middle of town, the Fraser River forms the southern boundary and the Pitt River runs along the east. The Traboulay PoCo Trail, a roughly 25-kilometre loop that locals treat as the city's front porch, follows those waterways past Gates Park and the downtown core around Shaughnessy Street. For a {BUSINESS_TYPE}, all that water is not just scenery. Metro Vancouver's heavy November-to-March rainfall lands hard on a city built partly on low river flats, so drainage, moisture and moss and algae on exteriors are constant realities here, and {COMPANY_NAME} plans every {SERVICE} job around them. A hillside {PROPERTY_TYPE} in Citadel Heights sheds water differently than an older home near the rail yards, and treating them the same is how corners get cut.

PoCo is also a commuter city with local pride. The West Coast Express links downtown Port Coquitlam to Vancouver, 25 km west, while Fremont Village has grown into the east side's shopping hub. And this is, famously, the hometown of Terry Fox, a fact stitched into the city's identity and its annual runs. {COMPANY_NAME} brings that same neighbourhood-level knowledge to {SERVICE_CATEGORY} work: {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} With {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of experience and a {RATING} rating, {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} From Lincoln Park to the Fraser flats, when Port Coquitlam needs {SERVICE}, the call is {PHONE}.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 61,498 (2021 Census, Statistics Canada)
- Incorporated in 1913 as a Canadian Pacific Railway yard town; rail lands still line the Fraser River
- Framed by three rivers: the Coquitlam River bisects the city, with the Fraser to the south and the Pitt to the east
- Hometown of Terry Fox, whose Marathon of Hope legacy is celebrated citywide
- The Traboulay PoCo Trail, a roughly 25 km loop, circles the city along its rivers
- About 25 km east of Vancouver in Metro Vancouver's Tri-Cities, with West Coast Express commuter rail service
- Postal code area FSA V3B; neighbourhoods include Mary Hill, Citadel Heights, Oxford Heights, Glenwood and Riverwood
